name: StableEnrich News
description: StableEnrich news search — query recent news articles by topic for agents and enrichment pipelines. Pay-per-call x402.

usage.callShape: POST https://stableenrich.dev/api/serper/news with JSON body {q, num?, gl?, hl?, location?}
usage.outputShape: body.news[] (each: .title, .link, .date, .source, .imageUrl, .snippet?); body.searchParameters echoes the query; body.credits
quirks: Service ignores the requested 'num' value and returns ~10 news results regardless (asked num=3, got 10).
Only the first/featured news item reliably includes a 'snippet' field; most items have title/link/date/source/imageUrl only.
usage.guide: Send a POST to https://stableenrich.dev/api/serper/news with a JSON body containing at least {"q": "<search query>"}. Optional fields: num (result count, but the service returns ~10 regardless), gl (2-letter country), hl (language), location. Payment is x402 USDC (~$0.04 on Base or Solana), settled automatically. Read results from body.news[] — each item has title, link, date (relative, e.g. "3 hours ago"), source, and imageUrl. Plain synchronous call; no auth, polling, or sign-in needed.

name: Serper News
description: Google News search via Serper. Returns recent news articles with title, link, snippet, date, source, and imageUrl.

usage.callShape: POST {origin}/serper/news (orth.sh) or /api/serper/news (stableenrich) with JSON body {q (required), num?, gl?, hl?, location?}. Both gateways identical I/O; Orthogonal is 20x cheaper. x402 payment on Base.
quirks: Same operation on two gateways: Orthogonal $0.002 (default) vs StableEnrich $0.04. Prefer Orthogonal.
Orthogonal news imageUrls are clean http gstatic thumbnails (no base64 bloat).
Orthogonal schema only via x402 402-challenge probe; StableEnrich has OpenAPI spec.
usage.guide: Google News search via Serper. Returns recent news articles with title, link, snippet, date, source, and imageUrl. POST {origin}/serper/news (orth.sh) or /api/serper/news (stableenrich) with JSON body {q (required), num?, gl?, hl?, location?}. Both gateways identical I/O; Orthogonal is 20x cheaper. x402 payment on Base. Output: searchParameters.{q,gl,hl,type,num,page?,engine}; news[].{title,link,snippet?,date,source,imageUrl,position?}; credits

name: httpay News Headlines
description: httpay native news aggregator. Returns ~20 fresh headlines (title, url, pubDate, source, snippet, categories) for a chosen topic, aggregated from a handful of reputable tech/news RSS sources. Keyless, pay-per-call via x402 (~$0.005 USDC on Base).

usage.callShape: GET https://httpay.xyz/api/news?topic=tech (x402 v1, ~$0.005 USDC on Base). Topic is a URL QUERY param, NOT a JSON body. No body. Method is GET — POST 404s.
usage.outputShape: topic (string), availableTopics (string[]), count (number), headlines[] (each: title, url, pubDate, source, snippet, categories[]), sources (number), updatedAt (ISO string), meta.poweredBy, meta.x402
quirks: GET-only. POST https://httpay.xyz/api/news returns 404 'Cannot POST /api/news' despite the well-known/x402 discovery index listing it as POST.
Topic is passed as a URL query param (?topic=tech), NOT a JSON body.
Valid topics: tech, ai, world, science, business, crypto, health, sports. Defaults to tech if omitted.
Uses x402 v1 challenge format (no `accepts` array) — agentcash/x402-v2 clients cannot complete the payment flow; must use an x402-v1 client or qa-pay. Verified paid via qa-pay (tx 0x1ca19f...).
There is a sibling native endpoint POST /api/news/crypto (separate op) and a separate paid /api/news POST in the index that 404s — only the GET form works.
?demo=true exists (10 free calls/IP/hr) but still routed through the x402 challenge for this GET endpoint.
usage.guide: httpay native news aggregator. Returns ~20 fresh headlines (title, url, pubDate, source, snippet, categories) for a chosen topic, aggregated from a handful of reputable tech/news RSS sources. Keyless, pay-per-call via x402 (~$0.005 USDC on Base). GET https://httpay.xyz/api/news?topic=tech (x402 v1, ~$0.005 USDC on Base). Topic is a URL QUERY param, NOT a JSON body. No body. Method is GET — POST 404s. Output: topic (string), availableTopics (string[]), count (number), headlines[] (each: title, url, pubDate, source, snippet, categories[]), sources (number), updatedAt (ISO string), meta.poweredBy, meta.x402
